back_translation: And it happened, when David and his men came to Ziklag on the third day, that the Amalekites had raided the Negev and Ziklag, and struck Ziklag and burned her with fire.
And they took captive the women who were in it, from small to great; they killed no man, but led them away and went on their way.
And David and his men came to the city, and behold, it was burned with fire, and their wives and their sons and their daughters had been taken captive.
And David and the people who were with him lifted up their voice and wept, until there was no strength in them to weep.
And David's two wives had been taken captive — Ahinoam the Jezreelite and Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite.

And David was greatly distressed, for the people spoke of stoning him, for the soul of all the people was bitter, each man for his sons and his daughters; but David strengthened himself in the LORD his God.
And David said to Abiathar the priest, son of Ahimelech, 'Bring near to me please the ephod.' And Abiathar brought near the ephod to David.

And David inquired of the LORD, saying, 'Shall I pursue after this raiding party? Shall I overtake them?' And He said to him, 'Pursue, for overtaking you shall overtake, and rescuing you shall rescue.'
And David went, he and six hundred men who were with him, and they came as far as the Brook of Besor, where those who were left remained.
And David pursued, he and four hundred men; and two hundred men remained, who were too exhausted from crossing the Brook of Besor.
And they found an Egyptian man in the field, and took him to David, and gave him bread and he ate, and gave him water to drink.
And they gave him a piece of fig cake and two clusters of raisins, and he ate, and his spirit returned to him, for he had not eaten bread or drunk water three days and three nights.
And David said to him, 'To whom do you belong, and where are you from?' And he said, 'A young Egyptian I am, a servant to an Amalekite man, and my master left me because I fell ill three days ago.
We raided the Negev of the Cherethite, and against what belongs to Judah, and against the Negev of Caleb, and Ziklag we burned with fire.'

And David said to him, 'Will you bring me down to this raiding party?' And he said, 'Swear to me by God that you will not put me to death, and that you will not deliver me into the hand of my master, and I will bring you down to this raiding party.'

And he brought him down, and behold, they were spread out over the face of all the land, eating and drinking and celebrating with all the great plunder that they had taken from the land of the Philistines and from the land of Judah.
And David struck them down from twilight until the evening of the next day; and not a man of them escaped, except four hundred young men who rode on camels and fled.
And David rescued all that Amalek had taken, and his two wives David rescued.
And nothing was lacking to them, from small to great, and to sons and daughters, and from plunder, and to all that they had taken for themselves — all of it David brought back.
And David took all the flocks and the herds; they drove them before that livestock, and said, 'This is David's plunder.'

And David came to the two hundred men who had been too exhausted to follow after David, and they had stayed at the Brook of Besor. And they went out to meet David and to meet the people who were with him, and David approached the people and greeted them with peace.

And every wicked and worthless man of the men who had gone with David answered and said, 'Because they did not go with us, we will not give to them from the plunder we rescued, except each man's wife and his children, that they may lead them away and go.'
And David said, 'You shall not do thus, my brothers, with what the LORD has given to us, and He has kept us, and given the raiding party that came against us into our hand.
And who will listen to you in this matter? For as the share of the one going down into battle, so is the share of the one sitting with the equipment — together they shall share.
And it happened, from that day and onward, that he set it as a statute and as a judgment for Israel until this day.
And David came to Ziklag, and sent from the plunder to the elders of Judah, to his friends, saying, 'Behold, for you a blessing, from the plunder of the enemies of the LORD.'
To those in Bethel, and to those in Ramoth-negev, and to those in Jattir,
And to those in Aroer, and to those in Siphmoth, and to those in Eshtemoa,
And to those in Racal, and to those in the cities of the Jerahmeelites, and to those in the cities of the Kenites,
And to those in Hormah, and to those in Bor-ashan, and to those in Athach,
And to those in Hebron, and to all the places where David and his men had roamed.
